_metadata.description: Complete merged reaction dataset — all 34 synthesis routes extracted from Chemistry by Design PDF. Includes morphine (9 routes), quinine (7 routes), galanthamine (4 routes), aspidospermine (5 routes), aspidospermidine (9 routes). Each reaction corresponds to exactly one page of the source PDF.

reactions.reaction_type: Cu-catalyzed enantioselective intramolecular O-alkylation / cationic cyclization
Friedel-Crafts acylation / intramolecular cyclization with PPA
partial reduction of nitrile to aldehyde

reactions.notes: Yield reported as 95% over 2 steps (steps 0+1 combined). Electrophilic aromatic iodination ortho to phenol.
95% yield over 2 steps (iodation + MOM protection). MOM protection of phenol.
Wittig olefination of aldehyde to give methyl vinyl ether (mixture of E/Z).
AcCl in MeOH generates HCl in situ. Simultaneous hydration of vinyl ether to dimethyl acetal and MOM deprotection of phenol.
Mitsunobu reaction coupling phenol with allylic alcohol. Inverts stereochemistry at alcohol carbon.
Intramolecular Heck reaction forming the C-C bond and establishing the quaternary carbon. Creates the benzofuran-fused ring system.
LiAlH4 removes Cbz group (reductive debenzylation) to give free amine; DNsCl then installs dinitrobenzenesulfonyl (DNS) group and N-methylation occurs. Two operations in one step.
Camphorsulfonic acid (CSA) promotes selective hydration/deprotection of the TBS ether to reveal the allylic alcohol. 68% yield over 2 steps.
Dess-Martin periodinane (DMP) oxidation of allylic alcohol to enone.
TFA removes acetal protecting group; MsCl/i-Pr2NEt promotes elimination/cyclization to form the morphine skeleton C-ring (N-C bond formation and introduction of the enone). Key cyclization step.
Thioglycolic acid (HSCH2CO2H) with base removes the DNS (dinitrobenzenesulfonyl) protecting group from nitrogen via thiolysis.

HCl in CHCl3/dioxane: 1,4-addition (conjugate reduction) to give the allylic mesylate intermediate. This step actually corresponds to NaBH4 reduction to give allylic alcohol in MeOH based on the PDF sequence.
NaBH4 1,2-reduction of enone to give allylic alcohol with correct stereochemistry for morphine.
BBr3 demethylation of aryl methyl ether to phenol, giving (-)-morphine.
Enamine formation from 6-methoxy-2-tetralone and pyrrolidine with azeotropic water removal. No yield given; used directly in next step.
Stork enamine alkylation with tert-butyl bromoacetate. Enamine acts as nucleophile at alpha position; hydrolysis during workup reveals ketone.
Asymmetric Diels-Alder reaction. The ketoester acts as diene precursor (after enolization) with chiral acrylamide as dienophile. 52% yield over 3 steps. Establishes key stereocenters.
Triflic acid promotes retro-aldol/elimination and tert-butyl ester cleavage/decarboxylation cascade to give the exo-methylene bicyclic enone. 60% yield.
PhSeCl in CH3CN/H2O: electrophilic selenenylation with anti-addition; water acts as nucleophile to give beta-hydroxy phenylselanide with defined stereochemistry.
mCPBA oxidizes selenide to selenoxide which undergoes syn-elimination to give the diene. 71% yield.
Cs2CO3 in EtOH at 60°C: base-mediated isomerization of exo-methylene to give the endocyclic alkene (conjugated enone system). 57% yield.
mCPBA epoxidation of the alkene with facial selectivity to give the alpha-epoxide. 71% yield.
K2CO3 in MeOH/DCM: base-mediated epoxide opening with lactonization, followed by ring-opening to give the hydroxy acid with simultaneous Favorskii-type rearrangement. 85% yield.
TMSCHN2 (trimethylsilyldiazomethane) esterification of carboxylic acid to methyl ester. 76% yield over 2 steps.
Luche reduction (NaBH4/CeCl3) of ketone gives allylic alcohol with high 1,2-selectivity. CeCl3 coordinates ketone oxygen for chelation-controlled reduction. 85% yield.
TsOH in toluene/water at 0°C: acid-catalyzed intramolecular epoxide formation from diol (dehydration). 76% yield over 2 steps.
LiAlH4 in THF at 0°C: reduction of methyl ester to primary alcohol. 90% yield.

Mitsunobu reaction: primary alcohol reacts with N-methyl-DNS-amine (N-methyl-2,4-dinitrobenzenesulfonamide) under Mitsunobu conditions (DEAD/Ph3P) to install N-MeDNs group with inversion. 80% yield over 2 steps.
Singlet oxygen ([1]O2) ene reaction. TPP = tetraphenylporphyrin as photosensitizer; hv generates singlet oxygen which reacts with the alkene to give allylic hydroperoxide. 85% yield.
Triethylamine (TEA) promotes Kornblum-DeLaMare elimination of the hydroperoxide to give the allylic alcohol and alpha-keto product. 86% yield.
Tf2O with 2,6-lutidine converts allylic alcohol to triflate which undergoes E2 elimination to give the conjugated diene/enone system. Epoxide also opens. 80% yield.

PhSH/i-Pr2NEt removes the DNS sulfonyl group from nitrogen (Fukuyama deprotection) and simultaneous intramolecular N-alkylation forms the morphinan C-N bond completing the pentacyclic skeleton. 51% yield.
HCl in dioxane/CHCl3: protonation/isomerization step establishing correct stereochemistry at the ring junction (epimerization). Product is the thermodynamically more stable isomer. 90% yield.
NaBH4 in MeOH: 1,2-reduction of enone giving allylic alcohol with the correct (alpha) stereochemistry required for morphine C6-OH. 62% yield.
BBr3 in DCM: demethylation of aryl methyl ether to phenol, completing the synthesis of (-)-morphine. 51% yield.
